elements.title: Mariska Aldrich
elements.contents: ... that opera singer Mariska Aldrich had a prize-winning German shepherd (pictured) who excelled in police-dog competitions?

elements.title: have the constitutional right to overthrow their government
elements.contents: ... that Salvadorans have the constitutional right to overthrow their government?

elements.title: a medieval treasure hoard
elements.contents: ... that a medieval treasure hoard was found by a woman digging for worms to use as fishing bait?

elements.title: Pittsburgh Cycle
elements.contents: ... that August Wilson completed his ten-play Pittsburgh Cycle only months before his death?

elements.title: Manuel Aguirre
elements.contents: ... that Manuel Aguirre was the first Jesuit to become a naturalized citizen of Venezuela?

elements.title: a steam plant in Alabama
elements.contents: ... that a steam plant in Alabama complied with emissions regulations simply by building a taller smokestack?

elements.title: Yunan Nasution
elements.contents: ... that after founding a press bureau in Bukittinggi, Yunan Nasution was arrested, imprisoned, and upon his release was forbidden from staying there?

elements.title: Angry Birds spinoff game
elements.contents: ... that a counterfeit of an Angry Birds spinoff game was removed for containing malware?

elements.title: Cool Moose Party
elements.contents: ... that in 2010, a Cool Moose Party candidate campaigned on abolishing the seat he was running for, and received almost 40% of the vote?
